title: United Nations International Yoga Day Festival Brisbane 2015
description:
ASMY hosted the first United Nations International Yoga Day at Roma St Parklands on Sunday 21st June 2015.  Thousands of yoga lovers joined us to celebrate the gift of yoga on this beautiful, sunny Sunday afternoon. Yoga day is to increase peoples awareness of the real meaning of yoga. 

Yoga is the most ancient system for physical, mental and spiritual well-being in the world. The different systems of yoga have techniques and practices ranging from physical postures (asanas), to the deepest stages of meditation, all aimed toward helping a person achieve optimum physical, mental and spiritual well-being. The aim of all these yoga practices is meant to help an individual achieve a life of 
harmony, a balanced life. The Sanskrit meaning of the word yoga is union of the individual self with the Supreme Self  in a loving, harmonious relationship.

Find out about 2016 event at http://asmy.org.au/yoga-day-festival-brisbane/

title: Three Yoga Essentials for International Yoga Day | Talk By Krishna Bhatta |
description:
KKNKTV presents Three Yoga Essentials talk by Krishna Bhatta on international Yoga Day June 21st 2015.  We congratulate PM Narendra Modi for helping Yoga find its deserved place in the world.  Yoga featured from Siachen to Kanya Kumari today in India and in 190+ other countries. There is new awareness and a new future for us all through the new place of yoga in the world and in our lives.  
Patanjali is the father of Yoga as we know it.  The three Yoga essentials, Discipline, Mind control and Mantra Om have their roots in his teachings.  Om is the ultimate sound' where there is no sound in deep meditation, there is Om.  The same sound was heard as Amen by meditators in the west.  Om and amen have the same roots.
"By proclaiming 21 June as the International Day of Yoga, the General Assembly has recognized the holistic benefits of this timeless practice and its inherent compatibility with the principles and values of the United Nations."
BAN KI-MOON
United Nations Secretary-General

title: Yoga Nidra for Chronic Pain |  30 Minute Guided Meditation
description:
Get FREE Trauma-informed meditation scripts + a sound healing track: https://www.annswansonwellness.com/7-meditation-scripts

This yoga nidra for chronic pain 30 minute guided meditation will aid you in total yoga relaxation and encourage natural pain relief. Learn the benefits of yoga therapy including how you can use yoga nidra to reduce pain.
 
Through guided yoga nidra and other relaxation techniques you are working on adapting and breaking through old neural pathways or habits of the brain (similar to the yogic concept of samskaras). The intention behind this 30 minute yoga nidra  is to shift your neurobiology to help you deal with chronic pain better, which can mean feeling less pain intensity overall. This guided meditation for chronic pain will leave you feeling relaxed and rejuvenated. 
 
Ann Swanson Wellness is focused on making yoga for everyone and for you to be able to access yoga anywhere. Whether you live a life with chronic pain or are looking to grow in your yoga practice, this free meditation for pain relief video is for you and we hope that this yoga nidra relaxation practice serves you well. 

ALSO TRY YOGA NIDRA FOR MINDFUL EATING & BETTER SLEEP BY ANN SWANSON: https://youtu.be/Nvt-cZ6r-OU

--- 

📌 Order _Meditation for the Real World_ book with bonuses: https://www.meditationfortherealworld.com

📌 Join the Meditation Teacher Training: https://www.annswansonwellness.com/meditation-teacher-training

📌 Order _SCIENCE OF YOGA_ book with bonuses: https://scienceof.yoga

---

Ann Swanson is an international best-selling author of _SCIENCE OF YOGA_ and _Meditation for the Real World,_ with over 500,000 copies sold worldwide in 15+ languages. She has a Master of Science in Yoga Therapy, is a licensed massage therapist, and a Tai Chi for Health instructor who studies with Dr. Paul Lam.

---

title: Indians Stretch Together with PM Modi on International Yoga Day
description:
Along with thousands of other people, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi marked International Day of Yoga on Saturday by striking a few yoga poses on a ceremonial road in New Delhi. 

At the crack of dawn, a mammoth exercise to showcase India's cultural identity through yoga was initiated. Roads in the heart of New Delhi became a carpet for yoga practitioners for the first time ever.

Indians in 650 districts across the nation participated along with people elsewhere around the world. The Rajpath, venue for the celebrations in the Indian capital, saw massive security arrangements for the prime minister and other yogis. 

Indian ministers, bureaucrats and even Indian military personnel participated in the exercise. The Indian government hopes that the event, involving 37,000 people, will enter the Guinness book of world records.

The 64-year-old prime minister, who is both tech-savvy and an avid follower of yoga, last year set up the new Ayush Ministry to promote yoga, a move that has been viewed as an effort to use yoga as a conduit of soft power. 

"It was a brilliant experience that we are here as a team, as a country together, to be here and to experience such a wonderful feeling of being together. Yoga is not just about your health; it's about meeting so many people to be a part of such a beautiful environment," said Lalita, a yogi. 

"It was a proud moment for being an Indian, because I have seen other people have come from around the world. And, it's not only the world, ... within India, all the religions came together," said Dheeraj, a yoga teacher. 

For the Indian establishment, the large-scale exercise was partly an effort to reclaim ground following concerns that India is losing "brand yoga" because the discipline has surged in popularity overseas.

Critics, however, say that while promoting yoga will generate more business for teachers and gurus, India still has massive health problems that the government should address first.

description:
Government of India is reported to be contemplating an ambitious preparation to mark the first-ever International Yoga Day which is to be observed across the world on 21st June, with the AYUSH Ministry planning an unprecedented mass yoga demonstration in order to get the mega event registered in the Guinness World Records book. The 35-minute event is expected to be participated by 45000 people en mass at Rajpath in Delhi. Muslim leaders have opposed the program by linking Yoga asanas to religion. Union Minister Mukhtar Abbas Naqvi has appealed them not to link the International Yoga Day with religion.

1.21 - Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras - Practical application
description:
तीव्रसंवेगानामासन्नः ॥१.२१॥

॥1.21॥ Tīvrasaṁvegānāmāsannaḥ
॥1.21॥ Samādhi is nearest to those whose desire  and intensity of action for Samādhi is intensely strong.

———————————————————————————

Welcome to this video series on Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras . 

These audio recordings are crisp, short and to the point. Remember, it is easy to complicate any topic but equally difficult to simplify it. I decided to simplify it without compromising on its essence. Most important thing about these series is that it tries to bring forth the practical application of all the 195 Yogasutras. 

This text helped me make my life beautiful and so I had to upload it at the earliest because I knew it will help solve many of my listeners problems too and make their life beautiful. 

So, listen to it with an open heart, contemplate over one sutra everyday, apply it wherever and whenever possible and see the magic of the Yogasutras unfold. 

Happy learning my dear friends. Have a great life.

Ayush Camp 19th April 2015
description:
After the success of the previous two instances of the Ayush Day in May and September 2015, Embassy of India organized a third such event on 19th April 2015. 
The event was opened by Ambassador's speech highlighting the importance of the upcoming International Day of Yoga to the representatives of the numerous invited Yoga institutions. This was followed by Rector of Debrecen University, Professor Zoltán Szilvássy, and Professor Baghel, the Ayush Chair in Debrecen, giving an insight into the this ancient science and the planned establishment of a European Institute of Ayurveda.  
After this, the Yoga institutions remained to discuss the International Day of Yoga with Ambassador Malay Mishra, while the other guests went to the residence to participate in the programmes and speeches:
-Cleansing Yoga Techniques by Mrs. Melinda Irtl
-Talk on Homeopathy by Ms. Andrea Székely (CHE)
-Sahaja Yoga by Ms. Maté Sangita
- Importance of Ayurveda in the Spiritual path and Vyayam Yoga by Bhagirathi Upasika and Rurdrani Chaitanya
- Pranayam, Asana, Yoga Nidra – Harmony of Meditation in Practice (Yoga in Daily Life) by Mr. Karoly Kovacs Krishnanand
The event was concluded with lunch for the approximately 200 participants.

PM Modi Celebrate Yoga Day With Thousands
description:
For more WORLD NEWS "SUBSCRIBE" US


NEW DELHI:  Prime Minister Narendra Modi Sunday hailed the first International Yoga Day as a "new era of peace", moments before he surprised thousands in New Delhi by taking to a mat himself to celebrate the ancient  practice.

The PM led more than 35,000 people, including bureaucrats, students and soldiers, performing poses such as the half camel and cobra in a 35-minute mass outdoor yoga session beginning at 7:00 am  on the wide Delhi boulevard of Rajpath.

Organisers are hoping the event qualifies for the Guinness Book of World Records for the largest yoga class at a single venue.

The current title, according to the Guinness website, was set by 29,973 students in Gwalior, India, in 2005.

"Yoga is more than only physical fitness. We are not only celebrating a day but we are training the human mind to begin a new era of peace,"  PM Modi told the crowd at Rajpath.

He thanked the United Nations for adopting his idea for a World Yoga Day.

"This is a programme for human welfare, a tension-free world and a programme to spread the message of goodwill," said the premier, wearing a crisp white outfit with a national tricolour scarf.

PM Modi then surprised the crowds by leaving the stage, removing his glasses, and securing a spot at the front of the massive session to mirror the stretches, breathing, and meditative moves beamed on giant screens along the historic avenue. (Pics)

The Prime Minister, who credits yoga for his ability to work long hours on little sleep, had been scheduled only to make a speech at the event.

Aerial images taken near dawn showed Rajpath, or King's Avenue - which connects the  Rashtrapati Bhawan with the iconic India Gate monument -- as a sea of white with scores of people, clad in new Yoga Day T-shirts, bending and stretching in sync with the English and Hindi instructions.

title: Day 12: eka pada rajakapotasana/pigeon pose with forward fold
description:
Day 12 of #RunnersLoveYogaYINYANG is #ekapadarajakapotasana/#pigeonpose with a forward fold--forehead rests on the floor. 

1⃣ From updog, tuck the toes under of your left foot to bring weight into this leg. Exhale; send your right foot high to the sky. 
2⃣ Inhale to mindfully bring your right knee outside your right foot; plant this foot outside your hand.🔹Release left foot toes to your mat. 🔹 Pause, breathe here. Gently press your right arm into leg, leg into arm. 
3⃣ Stay at palms OR (only if this is working for you--do NOT worry if it is not) drop to forearms. Again pause and breathe here. ⭐️ Another modification: drop your right forearm onto a yoga block or cushion. 
4⃣  Move your right palm/forearm/block out of the way and "heel-toe" your front foot across the front of your mat. The more parallel your shin is to the front of the mat, the deeper the stretch. Adjust this to your body's needs--you can angle your knee more forward to make a triangle shape with your leg instead of keeping the shin parallel. Keep front foot a little flexed. 
5⃣ Send right hip forward and left hip back, as though lining up hips with a magnet. Press front of back quad towards mat. Use the grip of the palms on the mat to "pull" your chest through your arms. Pause for a few breaths here, then walk hands out onto the mat to release upper body towards floor. Rest forehead on the floor (or a block). Stay for :45-1:00 each side. Repeat for a total of twice per side.

What is Yoga Therapy? | How can it help you?
description:
Sign up for my newsletter for insights on wellness—from yoga and meditation to tai chi & beyond: https://www.annswansonwellness.com/

Yoga therapy is the process of empowering individuals toward improved health and well-being through the application of the philosophy and practices of yoga. The benefits of yoga therapy include increase in mobility, pain relief and overall mindfulness. 
 
The International Association of Yoga Therapists (IAYT) updated the official definition to: Yoga therapy is the professional application of the principles and practices of yoga to promote health and well-being within a therapeutic relationship that includes personalized assessment, goal setting, lifestyle management, and yoga practices for individuals or small groups.
 
Yoga therapy is an integrative field that works to compliment western medicine and it is also fueled by the growing body of scientific evidence to support mind-body practices.⁣

title: Live Be Yoga | Stories From The Road – Turning Point Center
description:
Could yoga be the answer to quelling the drug epidemic in Vermont and beyond?

The Turning Point Center of Chittenden County, a peer-to-peer drug treatment and recovery center that helps people in Burlington, VT, transition to healthy and safe lives, is tackling the opioid epidemic in a unique way — by creating a community recovery yoga program.

Meg Tipper has been leading the Turning Point community recovery yoga program for just over a year and is in recovery herself. We got a chance to spend time with Tipper and talk to her about the incredible way that Turning Point is using yoga as a tool for recovery. When you meet Tipper, you can instantly sense that her passion for yoga and the recovery community infiltrates every fiber of her being. She is radiant and beaming with joy.

When reflecting on her first yoga class over 40 years ago, during a time when she was consumed by alcoholism, Tipper says, “I relaxed without chemicals, which was huge. To discover that I could get to a place in my body and mind where everything turned off, without being drunk … it was a miracle.”

Investigate more about the effort here: http://turningpointcentervt.org/

100 US Cities To Host Yogathon On First International Yoga Day
description:
For more WORLD NEWS "SUBSCRIBE" US

WASHINGTON:  More than 100 US cities will organise a Yogathon on the first International Yoga Day to be celebrated on June 21, organisers of the event have said.

In the ambitious 100+ cities drive, 15 cities in New Jersey-New York area, seven cities in California, six cities in Texas and three cities in Ohio have already finalised the details of the Yogathon, said Overseas Volunteer for a Better India (OVBI) - the lead organisers - in a statement yesterday.

All major spiritual and Yoga organisations like Art of Living have joined the campaign, OVBI said adding that more than 50 organisations have joined the countrywide campaign to spread the awareness about Yoga.

Sunstone Yoga, a Yoga studio chain having presence in 13 locations in Dallas has come as a sponsor for the event.

Ethnic organisations like the Nepali Student Organisation and Food for Life Global - the world largest vegetarian food relief organisation has joined in as an event partner.

Among its other partners include the Indian Consulate in New York and Air India and Subway. The United Nations, in less than three months, declared June 21 as World Yoga Day on December 11 last year, following a proposal by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

description: The 2nd International Yoga Day was celebrated in Juba, South Sudan on 25 June 2016 at the Panorama Plaza with a Yoga Demonstration led by personnel of the Indian contingent of UNMISS. Mayor of Juba City Council Stephen Wani and Mr. Kwong Gatluak, former Minister of Transport, Govt of South Sudan were the Guests of Honour at the event. The 40 minute Yoga demo was preceded by a Video presentation of PM Modi’s message, followed by short remarks by Indian Ambassador and Mayor Stephen Wani. Others who participated included the Kenyan Ambassador Cleland Leshore, Ambassador Paul Okaro, Director General in Ministry of Foreign Affairs, as well as UN personnel, members of expat community, Indian Community and local South Sudanese in Juba.

title: Joint Press Conference by EAM and MoS(I/C) M/o AYUSH on International Day of Yoga
description:
Grand Celebrations on International Day of Yoga 

The Ministry of AYUSH is all set for the grand celebrations of the first ever International Day of Yoga to be celebrated world-wide on 21st June 2015. An hour-long programme will be held at Rajpath on the D- Day starting at 6.40 AM and will be attended by Hon’ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi along with Cabinet Ministers, Minister of States, MPs and also Diplomats/ foreign nationals. In all, over 35000 people are expected to take part including students, NCC Cadets, CRPF & Yoga Institutions. 

This was announced by the Hon’ble Minister of State(I/C), Ministry of AYUSH Shri Shripad Naik during the joint press conference on International Day of Yoga here today. 

Department of Post is bringing out Stamp on the International Day of Yoga and M/o Finance is bringing out coins of the denomination of Rs. 10 and Rs. 100. Both the stamp and coin will be released on 21st June 2015 i.e the International Day of Yoga, announced Smt. Sushma Swaraj Hon’ble Union Minister of External Affairs during the joint press conference on International Day of Yoga here today. 

The Minister said that out of the total of 177 countries which have given co-sponsorship, 47 countries are from Organisation of Islamic Countries. They have co-sponsored the event and expressed their desire to participate in the yoga sessions being organised in the Indian Missions. 

AYUSH Ministry has written to States to celebrate International Day of Yoga in a big way. All States have been requested to organize similar events at State / District /Panchayat Hqrs by performing asanas from 7 AM to 7.35 AM. 

Over 11 lakh NCC Cadets will perform according to Common Yoga Protocol and about 9 Lakh members of Armed Police Forces will perform in their respective field units. 

One NGO for each of the 651 Districts have been invited for Yoga Training for one month so that they could be ready for the demonstration and Seminar on 21.06.2015. 

There would be live telecast and webcast of the event at the Rajpath. Given the expected large participation at one place, the Ministry has applied to Guinness World Records for “Largest Yoga Demonstration/ Class at single venue”. 

A 20 second video animation spot on the logo has been prepared which is being telecast over Doordarshan and other Private C&S channels across the country. A booklet on Yoga Protocol and a 33 minute DVD of Aasanas which will be performed at the Rajpath of the D-Day has also been prepared by the Ministry of AYUSH. 

Later, the Minister of State(I/C), Ministry of AYUSH Shri Shripad Naik urged the common man to start practicising Yoga and contribute in the celebrations of International Yoga Day on 21st June, 2015 and support the mission of our Hon’ble PM Shri Narendra Modi. 
Courtesy:pib.nic.in

title: Sadhguru Addresses the UN - IDY 2016
description:
Here is Sadhguru's full address to the United Nations on June 20, a day before the International Day of Yoga 2016. Sadhguru was invited to be the lead speaker at a special event ‘Conversation with Yoga Masters – Yoga for the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)’.

title: SINGAPORE: WOMAN ATTRIBUTES HER LONGEVITY TO BENEFITS OF YOGA
description:
(13 Feb 1998) English/Nat

Exercise is universally accepted as a good way of keeping fit.

Some exponents claim that a regular exercise and balanced eating can even extend life  expectancy.

But to claim that Yoga could help you live one hundred years would seriously stretch  the imagination.

One woman in Singapore is ninety nine, and attributes her longevity to the benefits of  the ancient eastern discipline of Yoga. 

She's almost a hundred years old..... but she looks decades younger.

She walks unaided, needs no glasses and hears perfectly. In every way a picture of  good health.

And it's all down to the far eastern discipline of Yoga.

She sticks to a Yoga routine twice a day .... bending her body into torturous positions.

Waist crunchers, hip twisters, shoulder stands.... she performs the exercises with  ease.

It's an impossible feat for most people but for Teresa Hsu, it's simply routine - ninety- nine years old she's clearly defying the ageing process.

She says that Yoga, coupled with a strict diet of vegetables and fresh fruits are the  secrets to her long life.

But more than that, she adds, it's the loving thoughts she has for humanity that keeps  her going strong.

SOUNDBITE: (English)
"The loving thoughts that goes out all over the whole universe. I think that keeps my  mind sane and healthy, and that in itself keeps a person comparatively young. If I tell  people I am 99 years old, most people say 'I don't believe you.'"
SUPER CAPTION: Teresa Hsu

But keeping the body fit is only half the story. She's just as dedicated to keeping her  mind sharp as well with regular readings of philosophy books. 

Born at the turn of the century in China, Teresa never married..... she's turned down  numerous proposals to dedicate herself to bettering herself and helping others.

SOUNDBITE: (English)
"I say no, I have no time, because so much to do looking after poor people. Because  one family, look after three or four children, one man. But I've got such a huge family,  that's why I say no, my family is too big. Have no time for a small family."
SUPER CAPTION: Teresa Hsu

Teresa's family are the poor the elderly and the sick. Her tireless dedication to helping  them has earned her the nickname of 'Sister Teresa'.

In her younger days, she lived among the poor in the slums of Paraguay where she  worked as an unpaid nurse.

Today, in Singapore, she runs a Heart-to-Heart welfare service with a core of poor  families under her wings.

Manickam Thanaraja is a volunteer with Heart-to-Heart and is much inspired by sister  Teresa's passion for life.

SOUNDBITE: (English)
"She's a person who lives her life for other people and she seldom thinks about herself.  Somehow or other, she has a mission in this world and her life is to serve people and  never to think of her comfort."
SUPER CAPTION: Manickam Thanaraja

SOUNDBITE: (English)
"Love your neighbour as yourself. Love everybody because there's not one single soul in  the world who doesn't want love." 
SUPER CAPTION: Teresa Hsu

Teresa founded this hospice for the elderly in 1965.

She won a Reader's Digest Asian hero's award recently ..... but typically, she says  she's forgotten the honour, having given the monetary reward away.

ANANDA JI'S YOGA PROPAGATION IN  BENGALI 10
Unforgettable Moments - Naam Yoga Superclass 2015
description:
Born out of his lifelong desire to provide people from all walks of life with authentic tools for self-healing and to make a difference in the lives of others, Dr. Levry has implemented annual Global Gatherings for Peace and Healing, also known as Naam Yoga Superclasses for Peace. While Naam Yoga has helped thousands of people to heal personally on an individual level, the Global Gatherings for Peace and Healing offer Naam Yoga to all of humanity in the service of world peace and global healing.  These massive global meditation classes are free public events dedicated to peace.

The sixth and most recent Global Gathering for Peace and Healing took place on November 26th, 2017, where 318,000 people united in the massive Zócalo square of Mexico City, online around the globe, and via live television broadcast, to pray for love, peace and light and healing for the entire world. The seed for the first global gathering was planted in 2010 and the events have continued to build, flourish and attract an ever-increasing amount of participants and media exposure each year.  The second superclass took place in Zocalo Square in 2011 bringing together 12,000 people. The third superclass occurred in 2012 at the Revolution Monument and was attended by 15,000 people and the fourth class took place at the Angel of Independence in 2014 and had an unprecedented 21,000 people in attendance on-site. In the fifth class on November 29th, 2015, an estimated combined number of 100,000 people attended in-person and online, making it the largest gathering of its kind in history.

Leading media outlets from around the world from Asia to Europe to North and South America covered these historical events dedicated to peace, among them CNN, Fox News, Der Spiegel, Televisa, Huffington Post, Der Tagesanzeiger, Vanguard and Reforma Corazon De Mexico. Dr. Levry holds that when a certain number of people come together in meditation, chanting Naam and holding the vision of peace, the impact is squared by the number of people present.  The government of Mexico City, inspired by the positive results of these superclasses, requested Naam Yoga classes be taught to the general  public for free once a week.

ABOUT SIVANANDA CANADA
The International Sivananda Yoga Vedanta Centres is a non-profit organization named after Swami Sivananda, one of the most influential spiritual teachers of the 20th century and founded by his disciple Swami Vishnudevananda.

The mission – to spread peace, health and joy through yoga. All are welcome to come to our ashrams and centres to learn not only about how yoga can help maintain a healthy body but also how yogic philosophy, applied in daily life, can help maintain a peaceful mind and spirit.

In 1957, Swami Vishnudevananda on instructions from his guru, Swami Sivananda, came to the West to bring the teachings of yoga. He established the first Sivananda Yoga Vedanta centre in Montreal, Canada, in 1959 and to date there are close to 60 Sivananda locations (ashrams, yoga centres and affiliated centres) around the world. Over 26,000 teachers have been trained in the TTC programs.

The Sivananda Yoga Vedanta Ashrams and Centres are recognized worldwide for teaching yoga authentically, preserving its purity and tradition dating back several thousand years. They also offer a wide range of programs and even the possibility for spiritual growth through Karma yoga as a temporary or full time staff program participant.

Swami Vishnudevananda made the yogic teachings understandable and available to all by simplifying them into five points, showing specific ways to develop physical and mental health as well as spiritual growth through ~ Proper Exercise (Asanas), Proper Breathing (Pranayama), Proper Relaxation (Savasana), Proper Diet (Vegetarian), Positive Thinking (Vedanta) and Meditation (Dhyana).

Together with Swami Sivananda’s revival of the yoga of synthesis tradition, which integrates the four paths of Yoga (Karma, Bhakti, Raja, Jnana), a well-balanced Sadhana (spiritual discipline) is achievable by those sincerely seeking for a change in their lives.

॥1.45॥ Sūkṣmaviṣayatvaṁ cāliṅgaparyavasānam
॥1.45॥ The subtlety of objects extends to the Aliṅga-the unmanifest state of the primordial matter.

—————————————————————

Welcome to this video series on Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras . 

These audio recordings are crisp, short and to the point. Remember, it is easy to complicate any topic but equally difficult to simplify it. I decided to simplify it without compromising on its essence. Most important thing about these series is that it tries to bring forth the practical application of all the 195 Yogasutras. 

This text helped me make my life beautiful and so I had to upload it at the earliest because I knew it will help solve many of my listeners problems too and make their life beautiful. 

So, listen to it with an open heart, contemplate over one sutra everyday, apply it wherever and whenever possible and see the magic of the Yogasutras unfold. 

Happy learning my dear friends. Have a great life.

description: Yoga was first practiced in India as part of a Hindu belief system but also became integral to Buddhism as well.Today, it is practiced throughout the modern world as a way of de-compressing from stress and becoming spiritually centered.Now Colombia is using this ancient practice to try and help combatants and victims of its 50-year armed conflict, overcome the wounds of war.Correspondent Michelle Begue reports from Bogota and Medellin on a new program called “Yoga for Peace.”

How to do number 4 pose.
description:
For more yoga poses you can do wherever you’re working, please visit https://cle.clinic/3txx70x

Alignment Points: Sit tall at the front edge of the chair with your toes facing forward and feet flat on floor. Keep your ankles under your knees. Cross an ankle over your shin either under or above the knee. Or if above your knee, place a supporting foot under the supporting knee. Lengthen up and hinge forward with a lengthened back, leading with the heart (and chin). Hold for 3 to 5 deep breaths. Repeat on the other side.

Benefits of Pose: Stretches back and hip muscles, particularly the periformus muscle.

Demonstrated by Judi Bar, Yoga Program Manager for Cleveland Clinic.

Sun Never Sets On Yoga - A 24-hour Journey of Sun Salutations Around the Globe
description:
The Sun Never Sets on Yoga

As a prelude to the International Day of Yoga, enthusiasts from around 100 cities across the globe performed Sun Salutations at historic locations on June 13. The universality and the timelessness of yoga found resonance when yoga enthusiasts from around 100 cities rose with the sun and rolled out their mats in front of historic locations to practice yoga as a prelude to the International Yoga Day (June 21st). Titled, 'Sun Never Sets on Yoga' this magnificent event spanning five continents witnessed the world literally wake up to yoga. Setting pace from the land of the rising sun, Japan to Budapest to St Petersburg to London to New York to Rio De Janerio, the event saw enthusiasts flocking unique and historic locations in Istanbul, Beijing, Shanghai, Hong Kong, Tokyo, Sydney and Auckland among others. Many cities in Russia, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, South Africa, Canada, Lithuania Cambodia, the UAE, Oman, Turkey, Indonesia also followed the sun and joined in hallmarking the comprehensiveness of this ancient technique.

title: PM Modi To Lead Global Celebration Of Yoga From UN HQ, Shares A Video Message On Intl Yoga Day
El Día Internacional del yoga: ¿unión o desunión?
description:
Una práctica milenaria, herencia de la India para el mundo, fue reconocida apenas este año 2015 con una fecha específica para celebrarla: el 21 de junio, “Día Internacional del Yoga”. Cientos, miles de personas se congregaron en todo el planeta, incluido México, para unirse durante media hora sin más propósito que el de encontrar un poco de paz entre tanta agitación, mediante prácticas de respiración y “asanas”.
Pero, ¿acaso esta fecha, esta búsqueda de bienestar físico y espiritual esconde o suscita intereses otros que los que cabría imaginar?
El Dr. Adrián Muñoz, profesor-investigador del CEAA con especialidad en temas de India y el sur de Asia, analiza los antecedentes y desarrollo de esta celebración desde varias perspectivas, con sus aristas políticas, económicas y religiosas.

Producción: Programa de Educación Digital / Colmex Digital

title: Doug Dooley, Alliance member since 2013
description:
Doug Dooley joined the Alliance as an experienced Silicon Valley executive for companies such as Cisco, Inktomi, Intel, Nortel, Juniper, NetScreen, Neoteris, and Ringcube/Citrix. In 2014, he transitioned to Venrock as a Venture Capitalist where he’s focused his expertise on infrastructure and security investments. “There is something to be said about the Alliance, and how it allows you to elevate yourself to see more broadly, beyond your own company,” Doug said. He comments that VCs have to practice using their executive muscle to think about businesses in a different way, with a broader perspective. His Alliance participation has been extremely helpful to him, where he’s experienced a willingness to share ideas, support one another, think long term, and maintain an entrepreneurial spirit of taking risks that are worth taking, which are all ingrained in Venrock’s culture of innovation. 

Originally established as the venture capital arm of the Rockefeller family in 1969, Venrock partners with entrepreneurs to build some of the world’s most disruptive, successful companies. With a primary focus on technology and healthcare, portfolio companies have included Apple Computer, Athenahealth, Centocor, Check Point Software, DoubleClick, Endeca, Gilead Sciences, Idec Pharma, Imperva, Illumina, Intel, Millennium Pharma, Nest, SlideShare and Tudou.

description:
Common Yoga Protocol #yoga #yogaday #ayushministry
#yoga #yogaday #ayushministry
अंतर्राष्ट्रीय योग दिवस 2026 से 44वें दिन पूर्व का योग काउंटडाउन कार्यक्रम 8 मई 2026 को मोरारजी देसाई राष्ट्रीय योग संस्थान, आयुष मंत्रालय  के सहयोग से राधे श्याम मोरारका सरस्वती विद्या मंदिर (माधव कुंज, सेक्टर-4 सी, शताब्दी नगर, मेरठ) एवं सोशल फाउंडेशन द्वारा आयोजित किया गया।

इस कार्यक्रम में मुख्य अतिथि के रूप में माननीय श्री डॉ. सोमेंद्र तोमर, राज्यमंत्री, ऊर्जा एवं अतिरिक्त ऊर्जा विभाग, उ.प्र. सरकार, विशिष्ट अतिथि के रूप में श्री राजेन्द्र सिंह, राज्य सूचना आयुक्त, उत्तर प्रदेश सूचना आयोग ने भाग लिया। कार्यक्रम में 600 से अधिक छात्र-छात्राओं, शिक्षकों और गणमान्य लोगों ने हिस्सा लिया।

कार्यक्रम का मुख्य उद्देश्य विद्यार्थियों समेत जनमानस में योग के प्रति जागरूकता बढ़ाना और स्वस्थ जीवनशैली को प्रोत्साहित करना था।

How to do fan pose.
description:
For more yoga poses you can do wherever you’re working, please visit https://cle.clinic/3txx70x

Alignment Points: Sit tall at the front edge of your chair with your feet set firmly on the floor. Make sure your back is off the chair. Watch to make sure your spine is lengthened. Your heart is at center with your chin moving forward. Pull your shoulder blades back. If your shoulders are tight or there is pain, keep your arms low on the chair. Take deep breaths here for at least 3 to 5 breaths.

Benefits of Pose: This simple pose works as a heart opener. Stimulates immune system. Opens upper respiratory muscles. Stretches shoulders and arms. Increases awareness of the action of the shoulder blades. Aids in relaxation which can help with digestion. Stretches your belly. Relaxes your mind.

Demonstrated by Judi Bar, Yoga Program Manager for Cleveland Clinic.

If you liked the video hit like and subscribe for more!

title: Patanjali Yoga Sutras - 6 | Cyclic Creation in Sankhya Philosophy
description: Lecture by Swami Tattwamayananda.  Swami begins by reviewing the importance of love for the end to be attained to sustain practice, Abhyasa.  Then, he explains in brief the Sankhya systems division of creation into an inactive consciousness, Purusha, and the active unconscious material, Prakriti.  From Prakriti, the mental and physical universe evolve.  Then, they involve back into Prakrti in a cycle.  The three gunas – sattva, rajas, and tamas – of Prakriti are also explained.  Verses: I.12, I.13, I.14, I.15, I.16

description:
क्लेशकर्मविपाकाशयैरपरामृष्टः पुरुषविशेष ईश्वरः ॥१.२४॥

॥1.24॥ KleśaKarmavipākāśayairaparāmṛṣṭaḥ puruṣaviśeṣa Īśvara
॥1.24॥ Īśvara is a special Puruṣa, untouched by affliction, action, result of action or the subtle impressions thereof.

———————————————————————————

Welcome to this video series on Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras . 

These audio recordings are crisp, short and to the point. Remember, it is easy to complicate any topic but equally difficult to simplify it. I decided to simplify it without compromising on its essence. Most important thing about these series is that it tries to bring forth the practical application of all the 195 Yogasutras. 

This text helped me make my life beautiful and so I had to upload it at the earliest because I knew it will help solve many of my listeners problems too and make their life beautiful. 

So, listen to it with an open heart, contemplate over one sutra everyday, apply it wherever and whenever possible and see the magic of the Yogasutras unfold. 

Happy learning my dear friends. Have a great life.

title: DURGA MANTRA Chanting Meditation for Protection Against Negative Forces | Mantra Music
description:
DURGA MANTRA Chanting Meditation for Protection Against Negative Forces | Mantra Music

Durga Maa Mantra is said to have powers to eradicate all the negativity and negative energies around us and protect us. 
In Sanskrit Durga means someone who can not be defeated. This Mantra invokes her name to protect us from all the harmful energies around.

MANTRA + MEANING

~ Om Dum Durgayei Namaha ~

Om - The primordial sound, the sound of universe. The vibrations of the OM sound helps us to connect with the Creator, with that source from where we all came from

Dum - Its the seed mantra sound of Maa(Mother) Durga

Durgayei - Formal Sanskrit Pronunciation of Durga

Namaha - We bow to you, we surrender to that immense Shakti represented by Maa Durga.

~ Om Dum Durgayei Namaha ~
 
=====

☯ MANTRA MEDITATION MONTH

At Meditative Mind, we are dedicated in creating Music for Healing Chakras,  Chakra Chants, Healing Meditation Mantras, And Relaxation Music .

title: Moving Beyond Judgment: A Critical Step for Humanity
description:
Waking up is a shift from perceiving the world through our minds, to simply perceiving, no mind, although the mind still functions as a tool we use. We're no longer filtering the world through it. 

One clue we're coming from a place of separateness, or perceiving through the filter of our mind is when we judge others and the world, or ourselves. 

Can we take action on the social, political and economic front without coming from a place of judging?

Kara-Leah Grant | The Yoga Lunchbox | http://theyogalunchbox.co.nz/

title: Yoga day controversy, Muslims can take Allah's name in place of shlokas, says minister :NewspointTV
description:
After dropping 'Surya namaskar' from the official yoga programme to "avoid controversy", the government on Thursday said chanting 'shlokas' during 'International Yoga Day' was not "compulsory" and appealed to Muslims to participate in the event.

Shripad Naik, Minister for AYUSH, the coordinating ministry for the event at Rajpath on June 21, said Muslims can "take the name of Allah instead of reciting 'shlokas'" during the event.

While some minority groups have objected to the holding of the event by government, especially inclusion of Surya namskar, representatives of some Muslim organisations who met Naik today said those opposing yoga were "enemies of humanity" and yoga has nothing to do with religion.

description:

Alignment Points:  Check for proper alignment in body. Use a chair for extra support if needed. Engage your legs and core, and lift your heels off the ground while pressing firmly into balls of your feet. Exhale as you lower your heels to the ground. Gaze at a stationary focal point.

Benefits of Pose: Strengthens legs, ankles, feet and arches. Increases focus and awareness. Increases core strength.

description:
Southern African Development Community (SADC) Parliamentary Forum
Opening Ceremony
Durban, South Africa

The 14 Member States of the SADC, namely Angola, Botswana, Lesotho, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, South Africa, Swaziland, Tanzania, Zambia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Zimbabwe and the Seychelles will all converge in Durban for the 37th Plenary Assembly of the SADC-PF to be held under the theme "Industrialisation and Regional Integration in SADC: The role of Parliaments."

Migration and Development; Free Movement of People: Towards Regional Integration; and Diaspora Engagement, are amongst the topics to be discussed at the 37th Plenary Assembly of the SADC-PF 2015.

title: Meditation Mantra for Peace | Lokah Samatah Sukhino Bhavantu | Mantra Chanting Meditation
description:
Meditation Mantra for Peace | Lokah Samatah Sukhino Bhavantu

May all my thoughts, words and actions contribute in some way to the happiness of all beings. This is a prayer each one of us can practice every day. It reminds us that our relationships with all beings and things should be mutually beneficial if we ourselves desire happiness and liberation from suffering.


MANTRA + MEANING

~ Lokah Samastah Sukhino Bhavantu ~

lokah: location, realm, all universes existing now
samastah: all beings sharing that same location
sukhino: centered in happiness and joy, free from suffering
bhav: the divine mood or state of unified existence
antu: may it be so, it must be so (antu used as an ending here transforms this mantra into a powerful pledge)

=====

☯ MANTRA MEDITATION MONTH

At Meditative Mind, we are dedicated in creating Music for Healing Chakras,  Chakra Chants, Healing Meditation Mantras, And Relaxation Music .

title: International Yoga Day celebrated around the world
description:
Yoga fans across the world have gathered to mark the second International Yoga Day. In Chandigarah, Northern India, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi joined thousands of people to perform “asanas” or yoga exercises. There were similar gatherings in Kalahandi district in Odisha, eastern India, but also in Canada, Australia and even Afghanistan. 

In 2014, The United Nations General Assembly declared June 21st the International Day of Yoga following a call from PM Modi during his address to the UN General Assembly.

description:
#Vedanta #Advaita #AdiShankara #Hinduism #Oneness #Nonduality #Spirituality
Ishavasya is a Upanishad belonging to Shukla Yajurveda comprising 18 verses. It appears as the 40th adhyaya in Samhita. Ishavasya speaks about Karma, Vidya, Avidya and finally the truth that has to be realised. 
Here in this series Dr Karanam Aravinda Rao takes us through Ishavasya Upanishad. Dr. Aravinda Rao, a former Director General of Police, AP, has been a keen student of Vedanta. He holds a Ph.D in Sanskrit and he has been teaching the Gita, Upanishads and the Ramayana online.

Patanjali Yoga Sutras - 41 | Niyamas: Utilization of Mental Energy
description: Lecture by Swami Tattwamayananda.  The Chittam, mental reservoir, accumulates tendencies and impressions from all of our previous life cycles.  It is like a lake with many layers.  A great spiritual seeker who has become completely pure will be able to see to the bottom of the lake - the eternal present.  Aparigraha, total independence cleans up the lower layers of the lake.  Becoming indebted to others robs us of spirituality.  Saucha, external purity leads to detachment from physicality.  Internal purity means mastery of the senses and feeling inner completeness.  Santosha means inner transcendental joy even in the midst of scolding or doing unpleasant things.  Tapas leads to physical and mental powers, which must be renounced in order to function.  II.39, II.40, II.41, II.42, II.43

title: The Yoga Sutras of Patanjali by PATANJALI read by Various | Full Audio Book
description:
The Yoga Sutras of Patanjali by PATANJALI (c. 150 BCE - ), translated by  Charles JOHNSTON (1867 - 1931)
Genre(s): Philosophy

Read by: sid, Chiquito Crasto, David Lawrence, Uday Sagar, April Gonzales, KirksVoice in English

Chapters:
00:00:00 - 01 - Introduction to Book 1
00:05:58 - 02 - Book 1
00:44:04 - 03 - Introduction to Book 2
00:47:18 - 04 - Book 2 from 1 to 28
01:10:32 - 05 - Book 2 from 29th to the end
01:34:59 - 06 - Introduction to Book 3
01:41:00 - 07 - Book 3 from 1 to 28
02:09:46 - 08 - Book 3 from 29th to the end
02:46:16 - 09 - Introduction to Book 4
02:49:05 - 10 - Book 4

Yoga sutras by Patanjali is a seminal work in yoga, this book is more about control of mind and the true goal of yoga.  The sutras are extremely brief, and the translation in neat English makes it very easy for people to understand the ancient Sanskrit text.  It starts with the birth and growth of spiritual man through the control of mind.  In all, this is a 'all in one' book for yoga philosophy written by the master himself. (Summary by sidhu177)

More information: http://librivox.org/the-yoga-sutras-of-patanjali/

Dance for Humanity Presents: Hidden Warriors Fashion Benefit at Light Space Studios, BK, NYC on April 18th, 2015 
Film by Shay Motion & Mark Salner.

Weekend Retreat in Gorgeous 5,000sf Secret Gallery and 800sf Sanctuary Studio in Bushwick, Brooklyn. Celebrating Fearless Living, Love, Freedom, Empowerment, Art, Music, Dance and Radical Self-Expression, all while Raising Funds for Human, Animal and Environmental Rights ♥ 

- Human Rights: Human Rights Campaign

- Animal Rights: Skylands Animal Sanctuary And Rescue

- Environmental Rights: Water Collective 

1230am: Hidden Warriors Fashion Benefit Walk Introduced By Layne Robert.

Morning Celebration. Classes and DJ Line-Up Below.

1230am:

230am:

soundcloud.com/peterponyclement

430am: 
KInner G [Atlantis / ebb + flow]
https://soundcloud.com/k-inner

♥ Line Up - Morning Dance ♥

630am: 
The Beat Kitty [Wonderland / Kostume Kult, NYC]
https://soundcloud.com/djorangekrush

830am: 
Dayburn [House of Dayburn / Dawn of the Burning Sun / Black Rock Observatory]
https://soundcloud.com/dayburnmusic/sets/dawnoftheburningsun

♥ Arts & Crafts Transformation Station ♥ 

Body Painting by Vann Godfrey of Transformational Bodypainting.

Make Up, Hair, Costume Styling & Body Art by Amanda Bociek. 

Body Art by Liquidmatter NYC LLC. 

♥ Dress Code in Honor to this Spring's Earth Day ♥ 

Think flowers, butterflies, birds, animals, trees, branches, secret gardens and enchanted forests. Let the kid in you play and be creative with costumes, fabrics, headpieces, hair, make up, body paint, wings, accessories and more!! Creative effort is the only requirement! There is coat check and two dressing room areas.

Are you ready to play & impact all at the same time?! We are!!!! :) 

SOUND & LIGHTS PROVIDED BY Guillaume G Atlantis


PROJECTIONS PROVIDED BY LIV MAN

♥ MEET US ♥ 

Dance For Humanity, Burners For Humanity and Love For Humanity are global movements that strive to provide tools for all people to decondition their fears, master their love for themselves and all beings and awaken to their life’s purpose and inner calling so that they may be empowered to transform the world into a kinder, non-violent place. 

Dance For Humanity offers an array of conscious events, retreats, getaways and festivals that combine spirituality, music, dance, art, and radical self-expression through classes, workshops, exhibits, social groups and sharing circles that amount to a powerful higher conscious dance experience. 

Teachings focus on love, acceptance, approval, compassion and forgiveness for oneself and all beings in the world. 

Spiritual tools include positive self affirmations, manifestation, visualization, tapping, mirror work, yoga, non-violent communication, body/mind/spirit movement, 5rhythms, ecstatic dance, dynamic meditation, chanting, sacred sounds, pranayama, shamanism, past life regression, ayahuasca, angel work, energy work, reiki, chakra healing, acupuncture, physical therapy, kinesiology, cranial sacral, ayurveda, intuitive painting, and more. 

www.facebook.com/wedanceforhumanity
www.twitter.com/Dance4_Humanity
www.pinterest.com/dance4_humanity
www.wedanceforhumanity.com

We Are All In This Together. WE ARE ONE. Live Out Love. Not Fear.

Powerful Mantra for Meditation | Adi Shakti Mantra | Meditation Mantra Chanting
description:
Powerful Mantra for Meditation | Adi Shakti Mantra | Meditation Mantra Chanting 

Adi Shakti Mantra is sometimes also called Adi Parashakti Mantra and it literally means the First Power. Its a powerful mantra to attract infinite divine power and energy.  When meditating with this mantra, our intention is to attune and merge with the powerful vast divine energy and merge with that infinite source of all of us.


MANTRA + MEANING


Adi Shakti, Adi Shakti, Adi Shakti, Namo Namo
(I bow to the Primal Power)
Sarab Shakti, Sarb Shakti, Sarb Shakti, Namo Namo
(I bow to the all encompassing Power and Energy)
Pritam Bhagwati, Pritam Bhagwati, Pritam Bhagwati, Namo Namo
(I bow to that which God creates)
Kundalini Mata Shakti, Mata Shakti, Namo Namo
(I bow to the creative power of the Kundalini, the Divine Mother Power)


MANTRA MEDITATION TECHNIQUE


Posture : Sit cross-legged with spine straight
Eyes : Slightly open and focus on the tip of the nose.This controls the mind,
Hand Mudra :  Right palm rests on the back of your left hand, and rests in your lap.
Chant : Repeat the mantra in your head or Chant slowly along with this.
Time : 11 to 60 Mins

=====

At Meditative Mind, we are dedicated in creating Music for Healing Chakras,  Chakra Chants, Healing Meditation Mantras, And Relaxation Music .

title: 1.16 - Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras - Practical application
description:
तत्परं पुरुषख्यातेर्गुणवैतृष्ण्यम् ॥१.१६॥

॥1.16॥ Tatparaṁ puruṣakhyāterguṇavaitṛṣṇyam
॥1.16॥ Indifference to the Guṇas gained through a Knowledge of the nature of the Puruṣa is called as Para Vairāgya.

———————————————————————————

Welcome to this video series on Sage Patanjali’s Yogasutras . 

These audio recordings are crisp, short and to the point. Remember, it is easy to complicate any topic but equally difficult to simplify it. I decided to simplify it without compromising on its essence. Most important thing about these series is that it tries to bring forth the practical application of all the 195 Yogasutras. 

This text helped me make my life beautiful and so I had to upload it at the earliest because I knew it will help solve many of my listeners problems too and make their life beautiful. 

So, listen to it with an open heart, contemplate over one sutra everyday, apply it wherever and whenever possible and see the magic of the Yogasutras unfold. 

Happy learning my dear friends. Have a great life.

title: Celebrate Earth Peace through Self Peace on International Yoga Day, 21st June 2015
description:
Blessings from Yogiraj Sat Gurunath Siddhanath
 
The world is celebrating International Yoga Day on the 21st of June 2015, the summer solstice. In India it's considered an important day of transition from Uttarayan to Dakshinayan. The sun that was moving North pauses and starts moving South after this day, bringing in a period of creativity and deep contemplation after the activities of the period of Uttarayan. 

The practice of Siddhanath Surya Yoga on this day will bring great benefits to all.

Patanjali Yoga Sutra 1.14
description:
In the 14th aphorism of the yoga sutras, Patanjali describes the qualities of a strong practice.

sa tu dīrghakāla nairantarya satkāra ādara āsevito dṛḍhabhūmiḥ I 

Practice that is done for a long time, without interruption, with devotion and enthusiasm becomes firmly rooted and stable. It acquires a strong foundation indicated by the word dṛḍhabhūmiḥ in the sutra. This is a reminder that the tree of yoga bears fruit over a long period of time provided its roots are sustained by a solid practice.

title: YOGA UNPLUGGED | IMMERSIVE COMMON YOGA PROTOCOL | DAY 5 | IDY 2025 | 16 JUNE 2025
# IDY 2025 TGTWURJC Boys, Jinnaram Part 2 Video
International day of Yoga - IDY 2025
WIM HOF BREATHING W/ Exercise Heavier Lifts
description:
WIM HOF BREATHING
Experimenting with Exercise
80 Breaths 
Wim Hof Breathing w/ Lifts & Plyometrics

I did quite a bit with the WHM today.  This is a short video on the exercise stuff I have been doing. 

Today I did many rounds of breathing and stretching then wanted to see how the Method Worked with some more muscle taxing lifts. 

I did 80 Breaths (no retention)

SPLIT SQUAT 70lbs
10 Reps each leg 
4 Sets 
50 Breaths in between sets 

SINGLE ARM OVERHEAD PRESS 45b KB
8 Resp on first 2 sets, 6 Reps on last 2 sets
4 Sets
50 Breaths in between sets

Close it out with 1 set of 
JUMP LUNGES w/12lb Medicine Ball 

Man I was tired and these exercises really tax my muscles.  A split squat is no joke, and at 70lbs that is a lot for me.  I got tired in the press and had to go down to 6 reps. 
The breathing did not seem to help too much with the reps, I hit muscle fatigue either way.  However, where it did help is in the recovery time.  After 10-15 breaths I was fine, but continued on to the 50 then started again. 

When it came to the plyometrics (Jump Lunges) this is where the breathing helps.  My cardio was good, it was my quads that started to fail, and after doing 4 sets of split squats I am surprised they lasted for 40 lunges.  

Next workout I will try lighted weights with higher reps, or sprints to see how I fair.  

After all of this I had an Ice Bath

title: Naam Yoga Dance of Light featuring Blessing Naam - A guided healing meditation series!
description:
Download the Mantra Sheet and MP3 here: http://rootlight.myshopify.com/collections/music/products/blessing-naam-free-single-instant-download

Read about the Naam Yoga Dance of Light featuring Blessing Naam:
The whole cosmos is permeated by music and movement which form a unified whole. The Naam Yoga movements in the Dance of Light with Blessing Naam work with the Law that governs the relationship between sacred words and music. Doing Naam with these movements, along with the rhythm of the music, causes one to come into contact with the sacred, pure and powerful forces in nature that create and build the future. These movements infuse the body with the harmony, rhythm, music and geometrical figures contained within those forces.

These movements are based on the deep knowledge of the forces governing the human organism and the whole of Creation. They make the body healthy, bright and beautiful. Ancient mystics knew that powerful forces could be awakened through the combination of sacred words, music and movement. They discovered the hidden potential powers in words and music and learned to tap into these forces through rhythmic movements that corresponded to specific geometrical figures, and combined them with vibrating sacred words. Naam Yoga works with motion, rhythm and music because there is harmony and rhythm in the whole of Creation. Words and music deeply influence our core essence and activate our physical and spiritual forces. The intelligent combination of sacred words, tones and movements can have a miraculous healing effect upon the human body, thoughts and emotions. These healing movements even awaken within us new creative forces. In the ancient esoteric schools, the mystical songs were also accompanied by movements. Keeping with this tradition, the Blessing Naam practice is the science of harmonious movements and their relationship with human thoughts and emotions. Vibrating Naam while performing these healing movements has an enormous effect on both the practitioner and those watching. It is this precise combination of sacred words with music and movements in the Naam Yoga Dance of Light that leads to healing.

When you vibrate the Blessing Naam mantra you acknowledge that you are a Divine child begging for his/her Father’s Divine Love, mercy and blessings. By wearing the virtues of tender-hearted mercy, humility, gentleness, love, patience and kindness we automatically receive them from God. This is an irrefutable formula. It is a prayer that, when uttered with devotion and complete humility, cannot fail. With devoted use, this prayer will eradicate karma and clear the way for a life of Divine Grace and blessings.

The Naam Yoga Dance of Light with Blessing Naam practice heals and strengthens our spinal column. All the powers we can use to penetrate into the superior dimensions of nature are found in the spinal column. The spinal column holds us up. It is the center of our sacred temple (the human body), and a conduit of both physical and spiritual energy. As you vibrate Naam while working through the various postures of the Naam Yoga Dance of Light, the body is reinforced with pranic energy, causing movement to become smooth and easy. Moving throughout the Naam Yoga postures is intended to release our Spirit, work on the mind, and involve the body, with the aim of fortifying and reinforcing it from within. We exist both through our physical and mental bodies. When we perform a posture or stretch a part of our body while vibrating Naam simultaneously, it heals and brings relaxation to all cells, muscles, organs and body parts involved. As a result, this relaxation of the whole body frees up stagnant or stuck energy, allowing the pranic force to move about the entire body. The entire body profits and is thus invigorated and rejuvenated.

Naam Yoga works on the nerves, muscles and bones. Combining Naam and movement brings flexibility, suppleness and agility, leading to a healing relaxation. Combining the various movements and postures with Naam lets the Naam Yogi affect the physcial plane via the spiritual plane. The yoga postures in the Naam Yoga Dance of Light flow gracefully, lending themselves to the practitioner with ease, regardless of their fitness level, yet their youth and health-giving benefits are extensive. It is important to move gracefully with the breath in order to ensure good circulation. Furthermore, vibrating Naam while moving the body supports and enhances one’s physical and mental wellbeing as well as our spiritual awareness and growth. Naam Yoga works on the totality of the human being, synchronizing him/her again with his/her true place within this vast Universe.

title: How to do mountain pose.
description:
For more yoga poses to start your day, please visit https://cle.clinic/3tszsKl

Alignment Points: Stand tall with feet parallel and relax your tailbone down for a neutral pelvis. Lift up through the spine. Tuck your chin in slightly, and keep your head balanced over your shoulders.

Benefits of Pose: Increases awareness of posture overall. Lengthens the entire spine. Can help improve balance when practiced with eyes closed.

Demonstrated by Judi Bar, Yoga Program Manager for Cleveland Clinic.

Om Shanti Om is an ancient Vedic Mantra. Om is the sound of universe, the primordial sound. Shanti means peace in Sanskrit.

We spread the vibrations of OM, thus aligning everything inside out in the right vibration and contributing to the peace in this universe.
We should practice this mantra atleast once a week for deep inner peace and to spread peace around us.

Sirsasana, Shirshasana (sher-shah-sahn-ah), Sirshasana, or Headstand is an asana.
In the Supported Headstand (Salamba Sirasana), the body is completely inverted, and held upright supported by the forearms, while the crown of the head rests lightly on the floor.
Sirsasana is nicknamed "king" of all the asanas.

title: Common Yoga Protocol 2016 demonstration by CYTER team led by Yogachemmal G Dayandiy
description:
As per guidelines issued by UGC and Ministry of AYUSH, Government of India the International Day of Yoga 2016 is being celebrated at Sri Balaji Vidyapeeth, Pondicherry in a grand manner from 19-21 June 2016. As a prelude to this event, SBV YOGA FEST was celebrated on 25-26 May 2016 to create awareness of Yoga amongst the university student community.
SBV Yoga Fest 2016 got off to a colourful start at MGMC&RI on 25 May 2016 with its inauguration by the respected Vice Chancellor of Sri Balaji Vidyapeeth Prof KR Sethuraman in the presence of the Dean (Research and AHS) Prof N Ananthakrishnan and Deans of the medical, dental and nursing faculties of the deemed university. More than 250 medical, dental and nursing students as well as faculty members and invited guests are participating in the two day event that has been organized on 26 and 26 May 2016 as  a prelude to the International Day of Yoga to be celebrated worldwide on 21 June 2016.
The Common Yoga Protocol booklet prepared by Ministry of AYUSH was officially handed over by the Vice Chancellor to Prof AR Srinivasan (Registrar of SBV), Prof Ravishankar (Dean, MGMC&RI), Prof Carounanidy Usha (Dean, IGIDS) and Prof K Renuka (Principal, KGNC).

Earlier the CYTER team gave a detailed presentation of the Common Yoga Protocol being practiced daily at CYTER for mass demonstration on 21 June 2016 at the Pondicherry Beach front. Yogachemmal G Dayanidy, Lecturer CYTER explained the CYP practices while the Yoga instructors Smt Sarulatha and Sri Dhanushapnadeesh gave an excellent demonstration of all the techniques.

Patanjali Yoga Sutra 1.16
description:
In the sixteenth aphorism of the Yoga sūtras, Patañjali continues to describe vairāgya (detachment).

tatparaṁ puruṣa-khyāteḥ guṇa-vaitr̥ṣṇyam I

In this sūtra, Patañjali describes the highest level of detachment, paravairāgya. The practitioner has achieved a connection with the pure Self that is so complete that any kind of attachment does not even arise in the mind. The knowledge or khyāti of the puruṣa empowers the practitioner to act from a place of full and total connection where there is a total absence of craving for anything material in nature. This is supreme non-attachment.

title: 'Raj rishi' PM Modi has set example by doing yoga at Rajpath says  Ramdev   NewspointTV
description:
Yoga Guru Baba Ramdev on Sunday said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has set an example by doing yoga at the Rajpath, adding that the discipline is an offering by India to the world.

"Yoga is India's offering to the world. Prime Minister Narendra Modi today showed that he is a 'raj-rishi' along with a politician and set an example by doing yoga. If the west has given science to the world then India has given yoga and spiritual knowledge to the world. 21st century's mantra is going to be prosperity with spirituality, prosperity with peace and the basis for that is going to be yoga," he said.

Commenting on the issue of Chinese mats being used for the International Yoga Day celebrations, Ramdev said, "We hope that the organisers keep that in mind and use Indian made mats in future...Yoga will become a huge industry in the days to come. There is going to be yoga dress, yoga food, yoga mat and other such things, all these things will be made in India instead of China."

Prime Minister Modi has dubbed the International Yoga Day as the beginning of a new era of peace and goodwill, thanking all those who have contributed to the development of the great discipline.

Inspirational Interview with Yoga Teacher and Kore Movement CEO, Jennifer Glass
description:
Jen is on a mission to bring Wellness to the World™. Born and raised in Leawood, KS with roots in Laguna Beach, CA, Jen is the Owner and CEO of KORE MOVEMENT. She spearheads client partnerships and the KORE MOVEMENT Team. As a hobby she adores teaching Yoga for Kids and Private Yoga Lessons in Kansas City. She currently serves as Vanity Fair’s lucy Activewear National Yoga Ambassador. Her 15+ years of training in ballet, jazz, and martial arts has laid a foundation of movement in her life, which led her to be certified in Pilates and Yoga. She has her 300hr RYT with more than 1,000 teaching hours and is in the process of acquiring her 500hr RYT in Los Angeles, CA. Be sure to follow her on her social media channels:

After Muslim Protests, Surya Namaskar Out of Yoga Day Drill
description:
After Muslim Protests, Surya Namaskar Out of Yoga Day Drill 

The Centre has decided to drop 'Surya Namaskar' from 'asanas' to be performed by people across the country to commemorate 'International Day of Yoga' on June 21.

Subscribe to Times Of India's Youtube channel here: http://goo.gl/WgIatu

Also Subscribe to Bombay Times Youtube Channel here: http://goo.gl/AdXcgU

Social Media Links: 
Facebook : https://www.facebook.com/TimesofIndia
Twitter : https://twitter.com/timesofindia
Google + : https://plus.google.com/u/0/+timesindia/posts

'Download TOI app on Android & iPhone and WIN free recharge coupon worth Rs. 50/- from Paytm - http://goo.gl/AvRYmM

Times Of India's Official YouTube channel is managed by Culture Machine Media Pvt Ltd.

The Centre has decided to drop 'Surya Namaskar' from 'asanas' to be performed by people across the country to commemorate 'International Day of Yoga' on June 21. The trimming of the drill is aimed at catering to the sensibilities of the minorities in the wake of opposition from Muslim groups. Launching a nationwide campaign against making what it dubbed "Hindu religious practices" compulsory in educational institutions, the All India Muslim Personal Board (AIMPLB) had demanded that the order making Surya Namaskar compulsory in schools be also cancelled. Surya Namaskar, a yogic practice for comprehensive development of the body, is considered a crucial component for improvement of holistic health. Earlier in April, the government had come under attack on the use of "Aum", a chant, while issuing a common protocol for celebration of the International Yoga Day. Meanwhile, preparations are on in full swing for the mega event on June 21, which is expected to witness almost 40,000 people from across the country performing 'asanas' at Delhi's Rajpath. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will address the gathering, minister of state for AYUSH Shripad Naik said. "Our ministry is also trying to register the mega event in the Guinness Book of World Records," Naik said. He said free yoga camps are being organized in 651 districts while 100 such camps will be held in Delhi to observe the day. External affairs minister Sushma Swaraj, along with Naik, will launch a special portal for the event on June 9. In December last year, the United Nations General Assembly adopted June 21 as International Yoga Day after PM Modi urged the world community to celebrate the ancient discipline on a global scale. With over 175 nations supporting Modi's proposal, the move was seen as major diplomatic win for India. Various yoga schools and centres like Isha Foundation, Patanjali and Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga are also collaborating to conduct workshops and yoga sessions along with showcasing different kinds of ayurvedic, herbal and organic products to visitors at the organic and wellness haat in the capital. The AYUSH ministry will also organize an international conference on yoga which will focus on holistic health. The theme includes traditional basis of yoga and its role in prevention of diseases along with therapeutic potentials of yoga. The conference will also discuss the importance of yoga for global peace. The railways has urged its over 13 lakh employees to celebrate the day in a big way.

With over 175 nations supporting Modi's proposal, the move was seen as major diplomatic win for India. Various yoga schools and centres like Isha Foundation, Patanjali and Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga are also collaborating to conduct workshops and yoga sessions along with showcasing different kinds of ayurvedic, herbal and organic products to visitors at the organic and wellness haat in the capital. The AYUSH ministry will also organize an international conference on yoga which will focus on holistic health. The theme includes traditional basis of yoga and its role in prevention of diseases along with therapeutic potentials of yoga. The conference will also discuss the importance of yoga for global peace. The railways has urged its over 13 lakh employees to celebrate the day in a big way.

"Dear Souls, as a server of humanity, I bring to you the message of the sacred Kriya Yoga given to us by the supreme initiator of humanity, Shiv Goraksha Babaji.  Babaji is the visible invisible savior of humanity.  He is called the nameless one and yet He goes by many names. And as He's beyond the comprehension of the normal ken of humanity He often accommodates to the limited understanding of us human beings and appears to us from time to time.  He has given to us a means whereby, a pathway to peace whereby we may realize the Divine Indweller within the depths of our own consciousness."

Sutra Mahamudra: How to Meditate on Emptiness w/ Ven. George Churinoff Workshop Begins - Nov 15 2019
description:
November 15, 2019

Begin 2:43


Mahamudra - "The Great Seal" - refers to emptiness itself and to certain very special meditative methods used to realize emptiness. Rather than emphasizing the philosophical meaning of emptiness as a preliminary, it strives from the start for personal experience of the clear light nature of the mind and then proceeds to meditate on and experience emptiness first hand.
The seminar will draw from the explanatory text and auto-commentary by the Panchen Lama, as well a presentation of that material in the recent book by Lama Thubten Yeshe, Mahamudra - How to Discover Our True Nature.

title: EXCLUSIVE : Narendra Modi's Speech At International Conference on Yoga at Vigyan Bhavan, Delhi
description:
PM Modi addressed an International Conference on Yoga organised by the Ministry of Ayush at Vigyan Bhavan in New Delhi.

Here are the highlights from his speech:
 
Today we are celebrating the first International Day of Yoga.
As I stand here today, the International Day of Yoga is being celebrated across the world, millions of people are participating in this event.
In India, this morning millions of people participated in a simple yoga demonstration at Rajpath (New Delhi), state and district capitals.
This coming together with our brother and sisters from other countries brings our hearts closer.
Yoga jeevan ko jee bharke jeeney ki jadi booti hai. (Yoga is the medicine to live life to the fullest)
The reach and spread of yoga spans continents and cuts across differences of colour, castes and creeds.
The body is a vehicle for the realisation of the Supreme Being.
The Gita says, Yoga is excellence at work.
Yoga is not an institution, it is a matter of faith.
If the body is a temple of the mind, Yoga creates a beautiful temple.
There is ample evidence that yoga helps in combating stress and chronic conditions.
A Yogi is a person who is in harmony with himself, his body, surroundings and nature.
Practice of Yoga leads to reduction of greed, coarseness and violence in an individual.
In a world of fear, Yoga creates courage and strength. A healthy body and disciplined mind is the foundation of a world without fear.
Yoga is not a sellable commodity. Yoga is for all humanity, we should not make it an exclusive preserve.

title: Yoga & Culture Retreat in Thailand - April 2017 - Pete Guinosso Yoga
description:
EARLY EARLY BIRD DISCOUNT ENDS MONDAY NOVEMBER 21st

SAVE $500 on FULL PRICE JUST BY PUTTING YOUR DEPOSIT DOWN BEFORE NOVEMBER, 21 2017

Join Pete for 10 days of exploration of the fascinating and awe-inspiring country of Thailand. We’ll begin at a highly-rated, luxurious, five-star resort situated on the edge of Krabi Marine National Park on the crystal clear waters of the Andaman Sea. Nestled discreetly amidst tropical gardens and coconut groves against a backdrop of sheer cliffs and lush jungle foliage, the resort can only be reached by boat. We will enjoy beautifully designed private bungalows, excellent cuisine, warm and attentive service and one of the most unique relaxation spas in Thailand. Yoga classes with Pete will bookend each day with ample free to relax by the gorgeous pool, snorkel in the stunning and serene ocean and simply be.

We will then fly to Chiang Mai, the cultural capital located in the foothills of the north. Custom designed excursions will include exploring ancient temples by rickshaw, participating in traditional offering at a Buddhist temple, enjoying a private walking and shopping tour of local artisans and their handi-crafts, cooking classes, an unforgettable traditional paper lantern ceremony and a visit to a nearby elephant sanctuary. Accommodation will be at a lovely property located in the historic heart of the city. This charming boutique hotel takes its name from a majestic 200 year old tamarind tree that shelters the hotel. The rooms reflect the rich ethnic diversity of northern Thailand by using fabrics and patterns drawn from various tribes of the region. Enjoy yoga with Pete, rich cultural interactions, delicious food and, as always, inspiring company… a memorable and moving wonderful introduction to all that Thailand has to offer.

title: Common Yoga Protocol for IDY 2016
description:
In 2015 the Ministry of AYUSH, Govt. of India made a film on Common Yoga Protocol to serve as an introductory guide to practicing Yoga. It contained, inter alia, general guidelines to be followed before, during and after the practice of Yoga and an explanation of the most widely practiced yoga poses and asanas. It was used by thousands of people across the world as a basic guide for the 1st International Day of Yoga.

The Common Yoga Protocol for IDY 2016 is an updated film made jointly by the Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga (MDNIY) and Ministry of AYUSH and includes more yoga techniques which have been added in consultation with leading Yoga experts and heads of eminent Yoga Institutions in India. This film will be a useful resource for the International Day of Yoga.

Dr Ananda Balayogi Bhavanani explores the theory behind "Pranava Pranayama"
description:
Dr Ananda Balayogi Bhavanani explores the theory behind "Pranava Pranayama".


The Pranava is the full cosmic Om (or AUM), represented as the three distinct vibratory sounds ofthe Akara  'AAA',Ukara  'UUU' and Makara  'MMM'. These three distinct resonant sounds are intimately tied with the three parts of the breath, vibhagha pranayama. The 'Ahh' sound originating from the solar plexus is related to adham pranayama, or lower lung breathing. The 'UUU' sound originating from the chest region corresponds to madhyam pranayama, or mid-chest breathing. The 'MMM' sound, as it moves upward and transcends its auditory character is associated with adhyam pranayama, or upper lung breathing. So then, the 'A' is verbalised as the air is being expelled from the lower lungs, the 'U' as the air exits the mid-lungs, and the 'M' as the remaining air leaves the upper lungs. 

As an added dimension of the practice, one can mentally 'think' these same sounds during the inhalation stage of the breath, as each section of the lungs is filled accordingly.

The Pranava AUM is practiced in conjunction with the mahat yoga pranayama, or the 'complete yogic breath'.  Pranava Pranayama involves a one pointed concentration on the Roopa and Nada (form and sound) of the sacred Pranava AUM-the Mantra of all Mantras.  When it is done with total awareness, then Pranava Dhyana a meditative state of being manifests, that harmonizes the body, emotions and mind. It is an important part of the Rishiculture Ashtanga Yoga tradition as taught by Yogamaharishi Dr Swami Gitananda Giri.


Dr. Swami Gitananda Giri maintained the healing power of the Pranava as unparalleled, while modern researchers and therapists continue to demonstrate its practical success upon patients with various afflictions.

The psychic benefits of the Pranava pranayama are immense. On the physical level, research has begun to reveal this practice as an incredible panacea, demonstrating impressive results for many conditions such as eye disorders, gastro-intestinal problems, stress disorders, chronic headaches, neck problems, anxiety, depression and a vast spectrum of psycho-somatic, mental and emotional disorders.

"After fifteen years of daily practice, experimenting and researching the practice of Pranava AUM pranayama... and having successfully tested it with all types of serious illnesses, mine as well as other students/patients, and having more than satisfied myself of its truth, I shared my knowledge and experience with the fervent hope that it may inspire serious, dedicated and disciplined colleagues to do likewise." ~ Sri Bala Ratnam, Vibrational Breath Therapy

Modi Government Selects NGO Run by Modi Government for Yoga Event
description:
Modi Government Selects NGO Run by Muslim Couple for Yoga Event

Amid criticism from its detractors that it is communalizing yoga, the Modi government on Monday selected an NGO run by a Muslim couple for holding a month-long yoga camp to mark the International Yoga Day on June 21. 

Subscribe to Times Of India's Youtube channel here: http://goo.gl/WgIatu

Also Subscribe to Bombay Times Youtube Channel here: http://goo.gl/AdXcgU

Social Media Links: 
Facebook : https://www.facebook.com/TimesofIndia
Twitter : https://twitter.com/timesofindia
Google + : https://plus.google.com/u/0/+timesindia/posts

'Download TOI app on Android & iPhone and WIN free recharge coupon worth Rs. 50/- from Paytm - http://goo.gl/AvRYmM

Times Of India's Official YouTube channel is managed by Culture Machine Media Pvt Ltd.

Amid criticism from its detractors that it is communalizing yoga, the Modi government on Monday selected an NGO run by a Muslim couple for holding a month-long yoga camp to mark the International Yoga Day on June 21. 

Based in Balrampur, the NGO, Mohammad Sayeed Memorial Welfare Society (MSMWS), works for health and educational issues of the under-privileged. 

MSMWS happens to be the only Muslim NGO to be selected by Union ministry of AYUSH's Central Council for Research in Yoga and Naturopathy. 

"We have been running Yoga sessions since 2013 after I went to Kerela and underwent a training session at Ispaa Academmy in Cochi," says Akhtar, who runs the NGO with his wife Shaista. At present, 100 members attend the sessions supvervised by the couple. Over 200 people are expected to take part at the Balrampur stadium event. 

Akhtar, 38, is not forcing Muslim participants to recite 'Om' while practicing Surya Namaskar and has accordingly directed his two instructors — Rupendra Srivastava and L N Tripathi. Muslim participants have been asked to either recite 'Allah', 'ayat' or any word they wish to or not say anything at all while practising Surya Namaskar and meditation.
